title: Anxiety + Depression Therapy in Leesburg, VA
seo_title: Anxiety & Depression Therapy in Leesburg, VA
subtitle: THIS IS FOR A SUBTITLE

blocks.title: Your chest gets tight before you even open your work laptop.
blocks.body:
Your jaw aches from clenching it in your sleep. You're exhausted but your brain won't shut up long enough to let you rest.

Or maybe it's the opposite. You feel nothing. You're going through the motions, showing up to sessions, responding to texts, but it's like you're watching your life happen from behind glass.

Either way, you're tired of feeling like this is just how it is now.

blocks.title: What Anxiety and Depression Actually Feel Like in Your Body
blocks.body:
Anxiety isn't just "feeling stressed." It's your heart racing when nothing's wrong. It's your shoulders living somewhere up near your ears. It's checking your phone seventeen times in an hour because your nervous system thinks something terrible is about to happen.

Depression isn't just "feeling sad." It's waking up already tired. It's the weight in your chest that makes everything feel harder than it should be. It's wanting to care about things you used to love, but not being able to access that feeling anymore.

Your nervous system is stuck. It's either running hot with anxiety or running cold with depression, and you can't seem to find the middle ground where you actually feel okay.

blocks.title: This Isn't About Thinking Positive Thoughts
blocks.body:
**The Brain-Body Disconnect**

You've probably already tried:

* Deep breathing (doesn't work when your body won't cooperate)
* Positive affirmations (feel fake when your nervous system is screaming)
* Exercise (helps temporarily but doesn't address the root cause)
* Meditation apps (can't focus long enough to use them)

The problem isn't that you're not trying hard enough. The problem is your nervous system has learned to stay in survival mode, and thinking your way out of it doesn't work.

blocks.title: How We Move Your Nervous System Out of Survival Mode
blocks.body:
I work with people whose bodies are stuck in fight, flight, or freeze. We don't just talk about your anxiety or depression. We work with what's actually happening in your nervous system that's keeping you stuck there.

EMDR helps your brain process the experiences that taught your nervous system to stay on high alert or shut down completely. Nervous system regulation work helps you learn what safety actually feels like in your body, not just in your head.

items.text: Notice what's happening in your body before anxiety takes over
Build capacity to feel emotions without getting overwhelmed by them
Shift out of freeze when depression makes everything feel impossible
Regulate yourself without needing to control everything around you
Trust your body's signals instead of fighting them

blocks.title: Your Nervous System Is Trying to Protect You
blocks.body:
Your anxiety makes sense. Your depression makes sense. They're your nervous system's way of trying to protect you based on what it learned was necessary to survive.

But what was protective before is now getting in the way of the life you actually want to live.

Therapy with me isn't about managing symptoms forever. It's about helping your nervous system learn it doesn't have to work this hard anymore.

blocks.title: Why Anxiety Work Benefits From Extended Time
blocks.body:
Here's what happens when we try to fit real anxiety work into 50 minutes:

We spend the first chunk of the session with you explaining what triggered you this week. By the time we get to the actual nervous system regulation work, we have maybe 20 minutes left. Just enough time to touch the surface, not enough to actually shift your baseline anxiety response.

You leave feeling slightly better, but still carrying the same hypervigilance you walked in with.

items.text: Actually practice nervous system regulation until your body learns the new response
Address multiple anxiety triggers in one session instead of leaving half-processed
Work through panic response patterns completely instead of just identifying them
Build capacity to stay present with discomfort instead of just talking about it

blocks.title: This isn't therapy that needs more time because it's "deeper."
blocks.body:
It's therapy that requires more time because we're literally retraining your nervous system, and that doesn't happen in soundbites.

\[Link to Session Options page]

blocks.title: Ready to Feel Less at War With Your Own Body?
blocks.body: Ready to feel more regulated and less at war with your own body? Let's talk about what's actually keeping you stuck.
